briefdescription.para: Logging severity level, ordered from most to least verbose.

detaileddescription.para: Levels are ordered so that level >= threshold is the enabled test. kOff is the maximum sentinel: as a threshold it disables all emission (it is never the level of an actual message).

briefdescription.para: The manifest content is data.
The manifest content is deletes.
The type of files tracked by the manifest, either data or delete files; 0 for all v1 manifests.
Unit for a Counter metric.
The type of a metrics report.
Time units supported by Timer.
Error types for iceberg.

detaileddescription.para: Branches are mutable named references that can be updated by committing a new snapshot as the branch’s referenced snapshot using the Commit Conflict Resolution and Retry procedures.

briefdescription.para: Tags are labels for individual snapshots.
The type of snapshot reference.
Ascending.
Descending.
Sort direction in a partition, either ascending or descending.
Nulls are sorted first.
Nulls are sorted last.
Whether a transaction creates a new table or updates an existing one.

detaileddescription.para: Used to represent some customized transform that can't be recognized or supported now.

briefdescription.para: Equal to source value, unmodified.
Hash of value, mod N
Value truncated to width W
Extract a date or timestamp year, as years from 1970.
Extract a date or timestamp month, as months from 1970-01.
Extract a date or timestamp day, as days from 1970-01-01.
Extract a timestamp hour, as hours from 1970-01-01 00:00:00.
Always produces null
Transform types used for partitioning.
A data type.

detaileddescription.para: This is not a complete data type by itself because some types are nested and/or parameterized.
Iceberg V3's unknown type is supported as a null-only placeholder type.

briefdescription.para: The time unit. In Iceberg V3 nanoseconds are also supported.
The algorithm used to interpolate geography edges.
Skip all file cleanup, only remove snapshot metadata.

detaileddescription.para: Clean up only metadata files (manifests, manifest lists, statistics), retain data files.

briefdescription.para: Clean up both metadata and data files (default).
An enum representing possible clean up levels used in snapshot expiration.
Factory function to create a reader of a specific file format.
Factory function to create a writer of a specific file format.

memberdef.definition: using iceberg::FatalHandler = typedef std::function<void(const std::source_location&, std::string_view message)>
memberdef.name: FatalHandler

briefdescription.para: A hook invoked on the fatal-log path just before std::abort().

detaileddescription.para: Receives the fatal record's source location and already-formatted message. Runs after the record has been emitted and the logger flushed, so an embedder (JNI, a Python host, a crash reporter) can print a stack trace, flush its own resources, or translate the abort. It must not return normally on the expectation of cancelling the abort ICEBERG_LOG_FATAL always terminates; if the handler itself does not exit the process, std::abort() still runs after it.

memberdef.type: std::function< Result< std::unique_ptr< ManifestWriter > >(int32_t spec_id, ManifestContent content)>
memberdef.definition: using iceberg::ManifestWriterFactory = typedef std::function<Result<std::unique_ptr<ManifestWriter> >( int32_t spec_id, ManifestContent content)>
memberdef.name: ManifestWriterFactory

briefdescription.para: Factory type for creating ManifestWriter instances.
A metrics report, which can be either a ScanReport or CommitReport.

detaileddescription.para: This variant type allows handling both report types uniformly through the MetricsReporter interface.

memberdef.type: std::function< Result< std::unique_ptr< MetricsReporter > >(const std::unordered_map< std::string, std::string > &properties)>
memberdef.definition: using iceberg::MetricsReporterFactory = typedef std::function<Result<std::unique_ptr<MetricsReporter> >( const std::unordered_map<std::string, std::string>& properties)>
memberdef.name: MetricsReporterFactory

briefdescription.para: Function type for creating MetricsReporter instances.

parameterdescription.para: Configuration properties for the reporter.

simplesect.para: A new MetricsReporter instance or an error.

briefdescription.para: Duration type for metrics reporting (nanosecond precision).
Result alias.

memberdef.type: std::variant< std::monostate, bool, int32_t, int64_t, float, double, std::string_view, Decimal, std::shared_ptr< StructLike >, std::shared_ptr< ArrayLike >, std::shared_ptr< MapLike > >
memberdef.definition: using iceberg::Scalar = typedef std::variant<std::monostate, bool, int32_t, int64_t, float, double, std::string_view, Decimal, std::shared_ptr<StructLike>, std::shared_ptr<ArrayLike>, std::shared_ptr<MapLike> >
memberdef.name: Scalar

briefdescription.para: A scalar value depending on its data type.

detaileddescription.para: Note that all string and binary values are stored as non-owned string_view and their lifetime is managed by the wrapped object.

briefdescription.para: A time point in milliseconds.
A time point in nanoseconds.
Stand-in for the current sequence number that will be assigned when the commit is successful. This is replaced when writing a manifest list by the ManifestFile adapter.
Well-known Logger::Initialize() property keys.

detaileddescription.para: level is honored by the base Logger::Initialize (parsed via LogLevelFromString) on every backend. pattern is honored only by the spdlog backend; CerrLogger uses a fixed layout and ignores it.

briefdescription.para: Property key for configuring the metrics reporter implementation.

detaileddescription.para: Set this property in catalog properties to specify which metrics reporter implementation to use. The value should match a registered reporter type.

briefdescription.para: Property value for the noop metrics reporter.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::span< const int64_t > positions, PositionDeleteIndex &target, std::vector< uint32_t > &scratch)
memberdef.name: ForEachPositionDelete

briefdescription.para: Apply positions to target as deletes; semantically equivalent to calling target.Delete(pos) for each entry. Out-of-range positions are silently ignored. Sorted, mostly-contiguous input is fastest.

parameterdescription.para: Caller-owned reusable buffer for the bulk dispatch path. Cleared and reused per key group; retain across calls to amortize allocations. Pass a distinct scratch per thread when calling concurrently with disjoint target.

briefdescription.para: Returns a string representation of an expression operation.
Returns the negated operation.
Traverse an expression tree with a visitor.

detaileddescription.para: This function traverses the given expression tree in postorder traversal and calls appropriate visitor methods for each node. Results from child nodes are passed to parent nodes.

parameterdescription.para: The return type produced by the visitor
The visitor type (must derive from ExpressionVisitor<R>)
The expression to traverse
The visitor to use for traversal

simplesect.para: The result produced by the visitor for the root expression node

briefdescription.para: Convert a FileFormatType to a string.
Convert a string to a FileFormatType.
String representation of a LogLevel.
Parse a LogLevel from a string (case-insensitive).

parameterdescription.para: The string to parse ("trace", "debug", "info", "warn", "error", "critical", "fatal", or "off").

simplesect.para: The LogLevel, or an InvalidArgument error if unrecognized.

briefdescription.para: Return the process-global default logger (never null).

detaileddescription.para: Off the hot path acquires the slot lock and returns an owning copy. The logging path uses the cheaper internal hot-path accessor instead.

briefdescription.para: Return the effective logger for this thread (never null): the active ScopedLogger binding if any, else the global default.

detaileddescription.para: Off the hot path returns an owning copy, e.g. to capture the current logger and re-bind it on a worker thread (see ScopedLogger). During teardown, prefer the Log(...) overloads over emitting through this handle.

briefdescription.para: Install a new process-global default logger.

detaileddescription.para: A null argument installs the no-op logger. Thread-safe; intended for occasional (configuration-time) use rather than the hot path.

briefdescription.para: Set the minimum level of the current default logger.

detaileddescription.para: Convenience for GetDefaultLogger()->SetLevel(level). Filtering is always decided by the logger's own ShouldLog(), so changing a logger's level by any means (this, SetLevel on a held handle, or Initialize) takes effect immediately.

briefdescription.para: Install (or clear, with nullptr) the process-global fatal handler.

detaileddescription.para: Thread-safe. Intended to be set once at startup. Replaces any previous handler.

briefdescription.para: Return the installed fatal handler (empty if none). Used by the fatal logging path; thread-safe.

memberdef.argsstring: (LogLevel level, internal::FmtWithLoc< std::type_identity_t< Args >... > fmt, Args &&... args) noexcept
memberdef.name: Log

briefdescription.para: Log to the process-default logger, std::format style. Formats only if the level is enabled; never throws.

detaileddescription.para: Example: iceberg::Log(LogLevel::kInfo, "loaded {} files", n);

memberdef.argsstring: (Logger &logger, LogLevel level, internal::FmtWithLoc< std::type_identity_t< Args >... > fmt, Args &&... args) noexcept
memberdef.name: Log

briefdescription.para: Log to an explicit logger, std::format style. Formats only if enabled.

detaileddescription.para: Example: iceberg::Log(logger, LogLevel::kWarn, "retry {}", attempt);

briefdescription.para: Get the relative manifest status type from int.
Get the relative datafile content type name.
Get the relative data file content type from int.
Get the relative manifest content type name.
Get the relative manifest content type from name.
String representation of a CounterUnit.
Parse a CounterUnit from a string.

parameterdescription.para: The string to parse ("count", "bytes", or "undefined").

simplesect.para: The CounterUnit, or an InvalidArgument error if unrecognized.

briefdescription.para: Get the string representation of a metrics report type.
Get the type of a metrics report.

parameterdescription.para: The metrics report to get the type of.

simplesect.para: The type of the metrics report.

briefdescription.para: String representation of a TimerUnit.
Create a name-based mapping for a schema.

detaileddescription.para: The mapping returned by this method will use the schema's name for each field.

parameterdescription.para: The schema to create the mapping for.

simplesect.para: A new NameMapping instance initialized with the schema's fields and names.

memberdef.argsstring: (const NameMapping &mapping, const std::unordered_map< int32_t, std::shared_ptr< SchemaField > > &updates, const std::multimap< int32_t, int32_t > &adds)
memberdef.name: UpdateMapping

briefdescription.para: Update a name-based mapping using changes to a schema.

parameterdescription.para: a name-based mapping
a map from field ID to updated field definitions
a map from parent field ID to nested fields to be added

simplesect.para: an updated mapping with names added to renamed fields and the mapping extended for new fields

briefdescription.para: Convert a Literal to a Scalar.
Project the expected schema on top of the source schema.

parameterdescription.para: The expected schema.
The source schema.

Whether the source schema can be pruned to project the expected schema on it. For example, literally a Parquet reader implementation is capable of column pruning, so prune_source is set to true in this case such that the from field in FieldProjection exactly reflects the position (relative to its nesting level) to get the column value from the reader.

simplesect.para: The projection result.

briefdescription.para: Get the relative snapshot reference type name.
Get the relative snapshot reference type from name.
Get the relative sort direction name.
Get the relative sort direction from name.
Get the relative null order name.
Get the relative null order from name.
Returns a string representation of a BlobMetadata.
Returns a string representation of a StatisticsFile.
Returns a string representation of a PartitionStatisticsFile.
Returns a string representation of a SnapshotLogEntry.
Returns a string representation of a MetadataLogEntry.
Get the relative transform name.
Converts a string representation of a transform into a Transform instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function parses the provided string to identify the corresponding transform type (e.g., "identity", "year", "bucket[16]"), and creates a shared pointer to the corresponding Transform object. It supports both simple transforms (like "identity") and parameterized transforms (like "bucket[16]" or "truncate[4]").

parameterdescription.para: The string representation of the transform type.

simplesect.para: A Result containing either a shared pointer to the corresponding Transform instance or an Error if the string does not match any valid transform type.

briefdescription.para: Get the lowercase string representation of a TypeId.

detaileddescription.para: This returns the same lowercase string as used by Type::ToString() methods. For example: TypeId::kBoolean -> "boolean", TypeId::kInt -> "int", etc.

parameterdescription.para: The TypeId to convert to string

simplesect.para: A string_view containing the lowercase type name

briefdescription.para: Get the lowercase string representation of an EdgeAlgorithm.
Parse a lowercase edge algorithm name.
Byte-swap a value. For floating-point types, only support 32-bit and 64-bit floats.
Convert a value to little-endian format.
Convert a value from little-endian format.
Convert a value to big-endian format.
Convert a value from big-endian format.
Write a value in little-endian format to a buffer.

simplesect.para: Caller must ensure output has at least sizeof(T) bytes available.

briefdescription.para: Read a value in little-endian format from a buffer.

simplesect.para: Caller must ensure input has at least sizeof(T) bytes available.

briefdescription.para: Write a value in big-endian format to a buffer.

simplesect.para: Caller must ensure output has at least sizeof(T) bytes available.

briefdescription.para: Read a value in big-endian format from a buffer.

simplesect.para: Caller must ensure input has at least sizeof(T) bytes available.

memberdef.argsstring: (int32_t num_retries, int32_t min_wait_ms, int32_t max_wait_ms, int32_t total_timeout_ms)
memberdef.name: MakeCommitRetryRunner

briefdescription.para: Helper function to create a RetryRunner with table commit configuration.
Compare two StructLike rows by scalar values.
Returns a TimePointMs from a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
Returns a Unix timestamp in milliseconds from a TimePointMs.
Returns a TimePointNs from a Unix timestamp in nanoseconds.
Returns a Unix timestamp in nanoseconds from a TimePointNs.
Returns a human-readable string representation of a TimePointMs.
Returns a time point in milliseconds that represents the current system time.
Index parent field IDs for all fields in a struct hierarchy.

parameterdescription.para: The root struct type to analyze

simplesect.para: A map from field ID to its parent struct field ID

This function assumes the input StructType has already been validated:
All field IDs must be non-negative
All field IDs must be unique across the entire schema hierarchy If the struct is part of a Schema, these invariants are enforced by StructType::InitFieldById which checks for duplicate field IDs.

memberdef.argsstring: (int32_t schema_id, const Schema &schema, std::function< int32_t()> next_id)
memberdef.name: AssignFreshIds

briefdescription.para: Assigns fresh IDs to all fields in a schema.

parameterdescription.para: An ID assigned to this schema
The schema to assign IDs to.
An id assignment function, which returns the next ID to assign.

simplesect.para: A schema with new ids assigned by the next_id function.

briefdescription.para: Check if type promotion from one type to another is allowed.

detaileddescription.para:
Type promotion rules:
unknown -> any primitive type
int -> long
float -> double
decimal(P,S) -> decimal(P',S) where P' > P

parameterdescription.para: The original type
The target type

simplesect.para: true if promotion is allowed, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Calls visitor with the corresponding concrete type class.

detaileddescription.para:
VISITOR
Visitor type that implements Visit() for all Iceberg types.
ARGS
Additional arguments, if any, will be passed to the Visit function after the type argument
Status
A visitor is a type that implements specialized logic for each Iceberg type. Example usage:

memberdef.argsstring: (const Type &type, VISITOR &&visitor, ARGS &&... args) -> decltype(std::forward< VISITOR >(visitor)(type, args...))
memberdef.name: VisitType

briefdescription.para: Call visitor with the corresponding concrete type class.

detaileddescription.para:
ARGS
Additional arguments, if any, will be passed to the Visit function after the type argument
Unlike VisitTypeInline which calls visitor.Visit, here visitor itself is called. visitor must support a const Type& argument as a fallback, in addition to concrete type classes.
The intent is for this to be called on a generic lambda that may internally use if constexpr or similar constructs.

briefdescription.para: Calls visitor with a nullptr of the corresponding concrete type class.

parameterdescription.para: Visitor type that implements Visit() for all Iceberg types.
Additional arguments, if any, will be passed to the Visit function after the type argument

simplesect.para: Status

briefdescription.para: Visit a type using a categorical visitor pattern.

detaileddescription.para: This function provides a simplified visitor interface that groups Iceberg types into five categories based on their structural properties:

listitem.para: Struct types: Complex types with named fields (StructType)
Variant type: Semi-structured type that is neither nested nor primitive (VariantType)
Primitive types: All leaf types without nested structure (primitive types)

detaileddescription.para: This grouping is useful for algorithms that need to distinguish between container types and leaf types, but don't require separate handling for each primitive type variant (e.g., Int vs Long vs String).

parameterdescription.para:
Visitor class that must implement five Visit methods:
VisitStruct(const StructType&, ARGS...) for struct types
VisitList(const ListType&, ARGS...) for list types
VisitMap(const MapType&, ARGS...) for map types
VisitVariant(const VariantType&, ARGS...) for the variant type
VisitPrimitive(const PrimitiveType&, ARGS...) for all primitive types
Additional argument types forwarded to Visit methods
The type to visit
Pointer to the visitor instance
Additional arguments forwarded to the Visit methods

simplesect.para: The return value from the invoked Visit method

briefdescription.para: Core Apache Iceberg C++ APIs.
